revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Funai-jinja (Nantan)]][[ja:船井神社]] {{Expand Japanese|船井神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Funai Shrine | native_name = 船井神社 | image = Funai-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Funai Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.104716|135.522243}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|de|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|it|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|mni|ꯁꯨꯃꯤꯌꯣꯁꯤ ꯁꯟꯖꯤꯟ|my|ဆုမိရောရှိနတ်သုံးပါး|pl|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|simple|Sumiyoshi sanjin|zh|住吉三神|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} }} {{nihongo|'''Funai Shrine'''|船井神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tanba Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kasuga shrine (worship)|lt=Kasuga shrine (worship)|qid=Q125316983|1=Kasuga shrine (worship)}} * {{ill|Q32512|ltq=Q32512|qid=Q32512|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11368560|ltq=Q11368560|qid=Q11368560|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tanba Province}} ** Series ordinal: 20 ** P155: {{ill|Q135040501|ltq=Q135040501|qid=Q135040501|1=Hietanono Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Shitahino Shrine|lt=Shitahino Shrine|qid=Q135040502|1=Shitahino Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q170421|ltq=Q170421|qid=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}} * {{ill|Q954511|ltq=Q954511|qid=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} * {{ill|Funai District, Kyoto|lt=Funai district|ceb|Funai-gun|en|Funai District, Kyoto|es|Distrito de Funai|fa|شهرستان فونای، کیوتو|fr|District de Funai|id|Distrik Funai, Kyōto|it|Distretto di Funai|ja|船井郡|ko|후나이군|pl|Powiat Funai|uk|Повіт Фунай|zh_yue|船井郡|zh|船井郡|qid=Q1078679|1=Funai District, Kyoto}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.104716|135.522243}} == image (P18) == * Funai-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|de|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|it|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|mni|ꯁꯨꯃꯤꯌꯣꯁꯤ ꯁꯟꯖꯤꯟ|my|ဆုမိရောရှိနတ်သုံးပါး|pl|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|simple|Sumiyoshi sanjin|zh|住吉三神|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182501 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ふないじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3130005007487 == Commons category (P373) == * Funai-jinja (Nantan) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府南丹市八木町船枝才ノ上51', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Funai-jinja (Nantan)}} [[JA:船井神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11613979}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Funai Shrine
(Funai Shrine) |画像 = [[File:Funai-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|250px]]
Honden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|qid=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}} Yagi-chō Funaeda Sai-no-Ue 51 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|6|17.22|N|135|31|20.51|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Funai Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
{{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Four Deities|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (tradition) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |本殿 = |別名 = Kanomori Myōjin / Kasuga Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = Sunday closest to {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|qid=Q2960|1=October 21}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Funai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Funai-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Funai Shrine''' (ふないじんじゃ; official name: '''Funai Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Yagi-chō Funaeda, {{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|qid=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} and a former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the old shrine-ranking system. The shrine crest is "tachibana" (mandarin orange). === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are the following seven pillars: * '''{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}''' — the deities enshrined since the shrine's founding; a collective name for the following three: ** Sokotsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuno-o-no-Mikoto * '''{{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Four Deities|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}''' — jointly enshrined in {{ill|lt=Hōan|Q1194464|qid=Q1194464|1=Hōan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1120|Q19542|qid=Q19542|1=1120}}); a collective name for the following four: ** {{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}} ** {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} ** {{ill|lt=Hime-gami|Q22070227|qid=Q22070227|1=Himegami}} === History === === Founding === When {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} carried out the {{ill|lt=conquest of the Three Korean Kingdoms|Q11249435|qid=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}, she prayed to Sumiyoshi Myōjin, whereupon a ship appeared and the campaign succeeded. Because of this, a shrine was founded at this location to enshrine Sumiyoshi Myōjin. About 500 meters northwest of the shrine there is a mound called "Oko no Mori" (Great Ancient Forest). It is said that the predecessor Sumiyoshi Shrine was originally located there, but was relocated to the present site of Kanomori (also "Kayamori")It is thought that the place name "Kanomori" (deer forest) arose because the Kasuga deity—associated with deer—was later jointly enshrined, giving rise to "Kasuga's deer forest," i.e. "Kanomori" ('Kyoto-fu no Chimei,' Funai Shrine entry). after a flood of the Ōi River in {{ill|lt=Keiun|Q428753|qid=Q428753|1=Keiun}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|qid=Q23504|1=705}}). The shrine and district name "Funai" is said to derive from the fact that this area was a mooring place (funai, "ship stop") for boats on the Ōi River ({{ill|lt=Katsura River (Yodo River system)|Q2909307|qid=Q2909307|lt=Katsura River|1=Katsura River}}). === General history === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, completed in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed as "Funai Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Funai District|Q1078679|qid=Q1078679|1=Funai District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|qid=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}, and was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. According to shrine tradition, in {{ill|lt=Ninna|Q1346317|qid=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} 3 ({{ill|lt=880|Q23618|qid=Q23618|1=880}}), the shrine buildings collapsed in a major earthquake and were rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Anna|Q1373003|qid=Q1373003|1=Anna (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=970|Q24047|qid=Q24047|1=970}}). In {{ill|lt=Hōan|Q1194464|qid=Q1194464|1=Hōan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1120|Q19542|qid=Q19542|1=1120}}), the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} was invited (kanjō) and jointly enshrined by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Ieyasu|Q11623187|qid=Q11623187|1=Fujiwara no Ieyasu}}, who was then provincial governor of Tanba. During the Edo period, the shrine was called "Kanomori Myōjin" or "Kasuga Daimyōjin"'Kyoto-fu no Chimei,' Funai Shrine entry., and the shrine's stone lanterns bear the inscription "Kasuga Deity." The main hall was destroyed by fire in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|qid=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1832|Q7584|qid=Q7584|1=1832}}) and rebuilt in Tenpō 6 ({{ill|lt=1835|Q7597|qid=Q7597|1=1835}}). After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Precinct === Among the shrine buildings, the main hall dates from {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|qid=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1835|Q7597|qid=Q7597|1=1835}}) and the worship hall from {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|qid=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1794|Q7811|qid=Q7811|1=1794}}). On the upper right of the inner worship hall, behind wire mesh, there is a horse ema said to have been made by {{ill|lt=Hidari Jingorō|Q2668220|qid=Q2668220|1=Hidari Jingorō}}. It is said that every night the horse would escape from the ema to go drink water; because the bell around the horse's neck was noisy, wire mesh was installed. Also in the precinct there is a five-tiered stupa called "Kainamori" (arm guard), said to enshrine the arm of {{ill|lt=Abe no Sadatō|Q3616500|qid=Q3616500|1=Abe no Sadato}}. Many mounds and place names related to Sadatō survive in the area around the Kameoka Basin (see "{{ill|lt=Abe no Sadatō#Tomb|Q3616500|qid=Q3616500|1=Abe no Sadato}}"). File:Funai-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|Precinct File:Funai-jinja (Kameoka) kainamori.JPG|Kainamori File:Old place of Funai-jinja (Nantan).jpg|Oko no Mori (former shrine site, by tradition) === Auxiliary shrines === * Hachiman-sha — enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Honda Wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Wakamiya-sha — enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshikumone-no-Mikoto|Q91088537|qid=Q91088537|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}} * Tenman-sha — enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari-sha — enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Uka-no-Mitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Izanagi-no-Mikoto / Izanami-no-Mikoto Shrine — enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * Taishakuten Yōhaijo — enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kyoto Taishakuten|1=UNKNOWN}} * Ise Jingū Yōhaijo — enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyouke-bime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-no-Ōkami|1=Toyouke-hime}} * Kainamori-sha File:Funai-jinja (Kameoka) setsumatsusha-1.JPG|(from right) Hachiman-sha, Wakamiya-sha File:Funai-jinja (Kameoka) setsumatsusha-2.JPG|(from right) Tenman-sha, Inari-sha, Izanagi/Izanami-no-Mikoto Shrine File:Funai-jinja (Kameoka) kainamorisha.JPG|Kainamori-sha === Major festivals === * Saitan-sai ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|qid=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen-sai (Sunday closest to {{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|qid=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Nagoshi no Ōharae (Sunday closest to {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|qid=Q2657|1=June 30}}) * Annual festival (Sunday closest to {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|qid=Q2960|1=October 21}}) * Niiname-sai / Harvest Thanksgiving Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|qid=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Toshikoshi Ōharae, Joya-sai ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|qid=Q2912|1=December 31}}) * Monthly festival (1st and 15th of every month) === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nantan|Q170421|qid=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}} Yagi-chō Funaeda Sai-no-Ue 51 '''Access''' * {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|qid=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} {{ill|lt=Yoshitomi Station (Kyoto Prefecture)|Q1044085|qid=Q1044085|lt=Yoshitomi Station|1=Yoshitomi Station (Kyoto)}}, approx. 7 minutes by car '''Nearby''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kyoto Taishakuten|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine origin document * '{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}}: Kyoto-fu no Chimei' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Funai District, Funai Shrine entry === External links === {{Commonscat|Funai-jinja (Nantan)}} * [https://funaishrine.wixsite.com/funaishrine Funai Shrine] — official site * [https://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#funaijinja Funai Shrine] — Nantan Life: Nantan City Comprehensive Guide {{神道 横}} == Categories Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]]
